config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. In my view, society is strongest when individual rights are protected first, because those rights limit abuse by governments and majorities. Of course, society needs rules and shared responsibilities, but those should not override basic freedoms too easily. From my perspective, once you make “society” the priority in a vague way, it can become an excuse to silence people and justify unfair treatment.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu diutamakan di atas hak individu, karena dari sudut pandang saya di Amerika Serikat, sejarah menunjukkan bahwa “demi kebaikan bersama” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Hak individu memang tidak absolut, tetapi justru hak-hak dasar itulah yang membatasi mayoritas dan pemerintah agar tidak semena-mena. Saya setuju bahwa masyarakat perlu aturan, pajak, kesehatan publik, dan keamanan bersama, tetapi itu berbeda dari memberi prioritas umum yang bisa menggerus kebebasan berbicara, privasi, dan kebebasan beragama. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat dibangun dengan melindungi individu terlebih dahulu, lalu membatasi hak itu hanya ketika ada alasan yang sangat jelas dan sempit.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over individual rights, because in my experience that is exactly how abuse gets justified. In Indonesia, I have seen how “for the public good” can be used to pressure people into silence, conformity, or unfair treatment, especially when the state or majority claims to know what is best. Society does need rules for health, safety, and order, but those rules should be narrow and accountable, not a blank check to override basic freedoms. A society is healthier when it protects individuals first and only limits rights when there is a clear, specific, and necessary reason.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena, dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika Serikat, memberi prioritas umum pada kepentingan masyarakat terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Sejarah politik di AS menunjukkan bahwa ketika negara atau mayoritas diberi ruang terlalu besar atas nama “kebaikan bersama,” hak bicara, privasi, dan kebebasan beragama justru yang paling cepat tergerus. Saya tidak bilang hak individu itu mutlak tanpa batas; aturan untuk kesehatan publik, keselamatan, dan ketertiban memang perlu, tetapi harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang menempatkan individu di bawah kolektif, melainkan yang melindungi hak individu dulu lalu membatasi hanya saat benar-benar perlu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still disagree with the statement, because in Indonesia I have seen how “society first” can easily turn into pressure from authorities or the majority against people who are different or inconvenient. That does not mean individual rights are absolute, since public health, safety, and order do matter, but those limits should be specific, transparent, and tightly controlled. When people say society should take priority in a broad sense, I hear a risk of silencing dissent and normalizing unfair treatment. A healthy society is one that protects individual rights as the default, not one that treats them as secondary.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena, dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika Serikat, menjadikan kepentingan masyarakat sebagai prioritas umum terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Saya setuju bahwa kesehatan publik, keamanan, dan ketertiban memang bisa membatasi hak tertentu, tetapi batasnya harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa diuji, bukan alasan kabur untuk menekan kebebasan berbicara, privasi, atau agama. Ketika “demi masyarakat” dipakai terlalu longgar, yang sering terjadi justru mayoritas atau negara menuntut orang diam dan patuh. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat melindungi hak individu sebagai dasar, lalu membatasi hanya saat ada kebutuhan yang benar-benar nyata dan spesifik.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
